senses.definition: a very small difficulty or problem that is actually part of a much bigger and more serious problem

examples.x: The financial audit revealed some irregularities, but that's just the tip of the iceberg.
The data we've collected so far is fascinating, but it's only the tip of the iceberg in terms of what we can learn.
The environmental impact study found pollution in the river, but that's just the tip of the iceberg when it comes to the overall pollution problem.
The scandal involving the company's CEO is only the tip of the iceberg; there's likely more wrongdoing beneath the surface.

senses.definition: used for referring to a list of people or items that is frustratingly long

examples.x: Everyone from the Prime Minister, to the Governor of the Bank of England, to the Mayor of London, to the Director of the IMF, to Uncle Tom Cobley and all convinced us that we needed these austerity measures to survive the recession.
I invited the entire neighborhood to the barbecue—Uncle Tom Cobley and all!
The company's new product launch was attended by journalists, influencers, and Uncle Tom Cobley and all.
They've invited politicians, business leaders, celebrities, and Uncle Tom Cobley and all to their gala event.

word_list.pronunciation: æz ɔːɹ fˈɑːɹ æz ðɪ ˈaɪ kæn ɔːɹ kʊd ɔːɹ wɪl sˈiː

senses.definition: until the point that one is capable of seeing

examples.x: The fields, as far as the eye can see, are filled with blooming flowers.
The city skyline stretches into the horizon, far as the eye can see.
Far as the eye could see, the sunset painted the sky in vibrant hues.
Far as the eye could see, the landscape was covered in a blanket of snow.
The construction cranes will shape the skyline of the city, as far as the eye will see.

senses.definition: affecting everyone or everything in a particular situation or group

examples.x: The company gave a pay raise to all employees across the board.
We saw improvements in test scores across the board for the students.
The new policy affected all departments across the board.
They received positive feedback from customers across the board.
The price increase impacted consumers across the board.

senses.definition: everyone, not just a certain group of people

examples.x: The town crier announced the news to all and sundry in the village square.
They invited all and sundry to the community picnic.
The store offered discounts to all and sundry during the holiday sale.
The public library is open to all and sundry who want to borrow books.
The charity event welcomes all and sundry to contribute to the cause.

senses.definition: with everything that one owns

examples.x: When they moved to a new city, they took all their furniture with them, bag and baggage.
She left her job, taking her office supplies and personal belongings with her, bag and baggage.
The family left the old house behind, taking everything they owned, bag and baggage.
The company decided to shut down the branch, moving the employees and equipment bag and baggage.
He decided to leave the country for good, selling his property and leaving bag and baggage.

senses.definition: used to convey how great the difference is between two things

examples.x: No city has escaped the recession, but Seattle has fared best by a long shot.
Oh, we aren't finished cleaning, not by a long shot—we still have the entire downstairs to tackle.
He won the race by a long shot; the other runners were far behind.
The new movie is better than the old one by a long shot.
Her score on the exam was the highest in the class by a long shot.

senses.definition: every single person from a specific group

examples.x: They called on every mother's son to help with the community cleanup.
In times of war, every mother's son was drafted to serve in the army.
They invited every mother's son to attend the town meeting and share their opinions.
In that storm, every mother's son sought shelter from the heavy rain.
The new law applies to every mother's son living in the county.

senses.definition: all of the ordinary people
a collection of many different things or items, often excessive or unnecessary, that includes almost everything that could be taken or brought along

examples.x: When they went on vacation, they packed everything but the kitchen sink.
The garage sale had so many items for sale, it was like they were selling everything but the kitchen sink.
He brought a backpack filled with snacks, supplies, and gadgets—everything but the kitchen sink!
Her presentation had so much information; it felt like she included everything but the kitchen sink.
They decorated the party venue with streamers, balloons, and all sorts of decorations—everything but the kitchen sink.

senses.definition: including every single detail

examples.x: She explained the topic from A to Z, leaving no detail out.

senses.definition: used when something happens or is done a lot

examples.x: During the storm, rain was coming down right and left.
She was giving compliments and criticism right and left, trying to be fair.
In the chaos, people were running right and left, trying to find safety.

senses.definition: to a great degree

examples.x: The flooding in the region was on a biblical scale, causing widespread devastation.
The storm's destructive force was on a biblical scale, leaving a trail of destruction in its wake.
The growth of the tech industry in the past decade has been on a biblical scale, transforming the world.
Their generosity in donating to the disaster relief fund was on a biblical scale, providing much-needed aid to those in need.

senses.definition: all that is possible, desirable, or available

examples.x: Send in the troops, aircraft, nuclear submarine experts, the whole nine yards.
She decided to go all out and plan the whole nine yards for her wedding, including a lavish reception and honeymoon.
He wanted the deluxe package for his car repair, so they replaced all the parts and gave it the whole nine yards.
When they organized the party, they went for the whole nine yards with decorations, entertainment, and catering.
The project required their team to research and present the whole nine yards of information on the topic.

senses.definition: a very great number of people

examples.x: The pub was so crowded that it seemed like the world and his wife had shown up for the game.
Their wedding was a grand affair, attended by the world and his wife.
The store's clearance sale attracted the world and his wife looking for bargains.
At the music festival, the world and his wife turned out to enjoy the performances.
The political rally drew the world and his wife, all eager to hear the candidate speak.

senses.definition: in every possible way

examples.x: She knows the subject matter to her fingertips and can answer any question.
As a chef, he is familiar with culinary techniques to his fingertips, creating exquisite dishes.
The musician played the song flawlessly, demonstrating his talent to his fingertips.
The detective's instincts and knowledge of the case were honed to his fingertips, helping him solve the mystery.
The athlete trained relentlessly, developing physical strength and agility to his fingertips.

senses.definition: anything and everything one can possibly think of within a certain category

examples.x: Gin, vodka, whisky, beer - you name it, I've got it.
I also enjoy windsurfing, tennis, racquetball, swimming, you name it.
We have activities for kids at the party: games, crafts, you name it.
The restaurant offers a wide range of dishes: pasta, seafood, steak, you name it.
Our company provides services for various industries: healthcare, technology, finance, you name it."

senses.definition: to add to the quality or intensity of something

examples.x: You may think your barbecue sauce recipe is good now, but trust me, this spice blend takes things up a notch.
The chef decided to take the spice level up a notch in the curry recipe.
To make the presentation more engaging, they decided to take the visuals up a notch with multimedia elements.
The team needs to take their performance up a notch to win the championship.
He wanted to take his fitness routine up a notch by incorporating more challenging exercises.

senses.definition: a rough estimate or calculation done quickly and informally, often using basic methods or assumptions

examples.x: We did some back-of-the-envelope calculations to estimate the cost of the project.
The engineer made a back-of-the-envelope calculation to determine the approximate load-bearing capacity of the bridge.
Before making a final decision, they performed some back-of-the-envelope calculations to assess the financial feasibility.
The manager used a back-of-the-envelope calculation to gauge the potential return on investment.

senses.definition: used to describe an examination of something that covers every aspect and detail

examples.x: It's time for a roots and all review of political donations.
When they cleaned out the garage, they removed all the clutter, roots and all.
The historian researched the family's history, tracing it back to the 17th century, roots and all.
The investigation aimed to uncover the truth, examining the evidence roots and all.

senses.definition: in a way that includes all the flaws and imperfections, without any attempt to conceal them

examples.x: Philip must learn to accept me, warts and all.
The biography portrayed the famous actor warts and all, highlighting both his accomplishments and personal struggles.
She described her relationship with complete honesty, sharing the good and the bad, warts and all.
The manager's report on the project was thorough, discussing its successes and failures, warts and all.
